item.title: Ukrainian woman named suspect in Monaco bombing
item.description: The suspect was initially identified as a heavily built person appearing to be male, but Interpol now says it is looking for a 39-year-old Ukrainian woman.

item.title: Maps show this July 4th could be hottest ever in parts of U.S.
item.description: Extremely dangerous heat, coupled with humidity, could result in heat index readings of 100 to 115 degrees from the Midwest to the East Coast, forecasters said.

item.title: Peace talks paused as Iran gears up for dayslong funeral for slain supreme leader
item.description: The flag-draped casket of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was put on display in Tehran Friday with millions expected to attend his dayslong funeral.

item.title: 40 killed when speeding, overcrowded bus tumbles into Pakistan ravine, officials say
item.description: A speeding bus plunged from a highway into a rocky ravine in Pakistan, killing 40 people and injuring eight others in one of the deadliest road accidents in recent years, officials said.

item.title: What's open and closed on July 3 and July 4? See which stores are operating.
item.description: Major retail stores will be open on Friday, although some may have modified hours on Saturday, July 4.

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding day arrives with MSG under tight security
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are expected to welcome around 1,000 guests at Madison Square Garden to celebrate their wedding, sources say.

item.title: "Vanishingly rare" copy of Declaration of Independence found in U.K.
item.description: A volunteer at the National Archives in London found the document among other papers seized from an American ship in 1776.

item.title: Trump says U.S. keeping current support levels for NATO would be "ridiculous"
item.description: President Trump says it would be "ridiculous" for the United States to continue its "one sided" relationship with NATO. His remarks came less than a week before a NATO summit in Turkey.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Security guard rescued from rubble 8 days after Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: Hernan Gil was brought out on a stretcher as elated rescuers cheered and hugged each other. He was loaded into a waiting ambulance and driven away.

item.title: Details emerge of Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding events at MSG
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding plans include a rehearsal dinner and a late-night celebration at Madison Square Garden in New York City, according to sources familiar with the security planning.

item.title: Aspen Acres Fire continues to burn out of control in southern Colorado
item.description: The Aspen Acres Fire continues to burn out of control in Pueblo and Custer counties.

item.title: Bessent on Trump's crypto earnings: "I don't think there's an appearance problem"
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent touched on the recent disclosure of President Trump's crypto earnings, the latest developments with the tax-deferred Trump Accounts, and the struggles facing the U.S. economy.

item.title: Exclusive interview: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent on Trump accounts, Trump's crypto gains, more
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent speaks with CBS News' Kelly O'Grady about stock donations for Trump accounts, the war in Iran, President Trump's $1.4 billion in crypto earnings and more.

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce donate $26 million ahead of wedding
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce donated to 20 local and national charities ahead of their wedding Friday.

item.title: What parents want 1 year after Camp Mystic flooding tragedy
item.description: Saturday marks one year since the catastrophic flooding that swept through Camp Mystic in Texas. Raging river water flooded the camp for young girls, killing 25 campers, two counselors and the camp's executive director. CBS News' Jason Allen reports.

item.title: Breaking down the constitutional questions in the transgender athletes case
item.description: This week, the Supreme Court upheld state bans on transgender athletes in girls' and women's sports. CBS News legal contributor Jessica Levinson answers some constitutional questions about the case.

item.title: Job growth slowed in June, falling short of expectations, latest report shows
item.description: Hiring in the U.S. slowed in June, falling short of expectations, according to the Labor Department. CBS News producer Emily Pandise has more.

item.title: Wedding festivities kick off for Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce in NYC
item.description: Wedding celebrations are getting underway at Madison Square Garden as pop superstar Taylor Swift and Super Bowl champion Travis Kelce prepare to celebrate their wedding. CBS News' Jo Ling Kent has more.

item.title: FBI investigating legitimacy of Nancy Guthrie ransom notes
item.description: The FBI said in a statement Wednesday that some ransom notes in Nancy Guthrie's disappearance have been "deemed to be extortion attempts without legitimacy," and other "demands may potentially be legitimate and are still being investigated as such."

item.title: Former CDC chief medical officer Debra Houry discusses her resignation over RFK Jr.'s policies
item.description: In a CBS News exclusive, "Face the Nation" moderator and chief foreign affairs correspondent Margaret Brennan spoke with former CDC chief medical officer Dr. Debra Houry, who resigned in protest after HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. fired then-CDC Director Susan Monarez. Brennan joins "The Takeout" to unpack the interview.

item.title: 7/2: CBS Evening News
item.description: More than 200 million Americans are under heat alerts; the couple who scaled the Empire State Building are faces criminal charges.

item.title: America 250 celebrations in Washington, D.C., deemed national special security event
item.description: Washington, D.C., is bracing for the heat and ramping up security in advance of the nation's 250th birthday celebration on the National Mall. CBS News homeland security correspondent Nicole Sganga reports on the security efforts. Then, Amb. Monica Crowley, U.S. chief of protocol and administration representative to Freedom 250, joins to discuss the festivities.

item.title: McConnell still hospitalized after EMS responded to his home last month
item.description: A spokesperson said the Kentucky Republican "continues to improve."

item.title: What the Founding Fathers said about the struggle against King George III, British Empire in 1776
item.description: In celebration of America's 250th birthday, CBS News chief Washington correspondent Major Garrett offers a small sampling of thoughts from 1776 about the Revolutionary War.

item.title: 7/2: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Security ramps up for D.C. Fourth of July events; taxes on Trump's $1.4 billion crypto income could total hundreds of millions, sources say.

item.title: Spotify removes streams of No. 1 song after suspicious Kalshi bets
item.description: Spotify said the streams tied to Malcolm Todd's "Earrings" were not from genuine listeners after suspicious betting activity emerged on Kalshi.

item.title: FBI orders field offices to send analysts to Atlanta for election probe
item.description: The FBI is asking for analysts to help evaluate thousands of records for a "priority" investigation ordered by FBI Director Kash Patel.

item.title: What a Brooklyn bodega reveals about the craze for an experimental weight-loss drug
item.description: It isn't approved by the FDA, but we found an experimental weight-loss drug called retatrutide for sale at a local convenience store.

item.title: How "Ted Lasso" became a television phenomenon
item.description: Jason Sudeikis and the cast and crew of "Ted Lasso" tell Norah O'Donnell about the show's improbable rise and positive message.
